proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (project_anchor §7 D-8 review); if exit logic is revised, cohort may re-tier
Bear regime has been absent for 7 days; verify Bear sample is not concentrated in a narrow window when cohort activates
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +30.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +30.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy with zero open exposure, so activation is deferred and concentration risk is not compounded. Consistent with prior approvals of this exact cohort on 2026-04-29 and 2026-04-30; no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +24.0pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.72 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (project_anchor §7 D-8); true edge may be understated but cap at TAKE_SMALL is appropriate until D-8 review resolves
Sample n=17 is near the Tier M floor — single adverse outcome cluster could trigger demotion on next archive comparison
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +24.0pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +24.0pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) and promotion is correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. Portfolio is currently flat (0 open positions) so no concentration risk, and the sector=Metal filter narrows rather than broadens UP_TRI exposure. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort and no trader rejection on record.
